revision.text:
{{Infobox Mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft}} | Range = {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|12=draft}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}} | Map Caption = Location of Mount Moriya }} '''Mount Moriya''' (守屋山) is a mountain with an elevation of 1,651 meters, located on the border between {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}.{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=Revised Elevation Values for Mountains|publisher=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|accessdate=2014-03-26}} == Name == The mountain was historically known as '''Moriya Mountain''' (森山). Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Locals in the Suwa region often refer to the mountain range that includes Mount Moriya as "Nishiyama" (西山), meaning "Western Mountains." == Overview == Situated at the northernmost part of the {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|12=draft}}, the summit provides panoramic views of the {{ill|Southern Alps (Japan)|ja|赤石山脈|12=draft}}, {{ill|Central Alps (Japan)|ja|木曽山脈|12=draft}}, {{ill|Northern Alps (Japan)|ja|飛騨山脈|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳連峰|12=draft}}. The mountain serves as the source of the {{ill|Sawakawa River|ja|沢川|12=draft}}, which flows into {{ill|Lake Momiji|ja|箕輪ダム|12=draft}} before joining the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}. The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩|12=draft}}, making it a geologically significant area where the {{ill|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|ja|糸魚川静岡構造線|12=draft}} intersects with the {{ill|Median Tectonic Line|ja|中央構造線|12=draft}}. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity {{ill|Moriya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}}, also identified with {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|12=draft}}. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Mountains associated with historical figures]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Mount Moriya]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[en:Moriya Mountain]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Moriya}}

Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Mountains associated with historical figures]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Mount Moriya]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[en:Moriya Mountain]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Moriya}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft}} | Range = {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|12=draft}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}} | Map Caption = Location of Mount Moriya }} '''Mount Moriya''' (守屋山) is a mountain with an elevation of 1,651 meters, located on the border between {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}.{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=Revised Elevation Values for Mountains|publisher=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|accessdate=2014-03-26}} == Name == The mountain was historically known as '''Moriya Mountain''' (森山). Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Locals in the Suwa region often refer to the mountain range that includes Mount Moriya as "Nishiyama" (西山), meaning "Western Mountains." == Overview == Situated at the northernmost part of the {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|12=draft}}, the summit provides panoramic views of the {{ill|Southern Alps (Japan)|ja|赤石山脈|12=draft}}, {{ill|Central Alps (Japan)|ja|木曽山脈|12=draft}}, {{ill|Northern Alps (Japan)|ja|飛騨山脈|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳連峰|12=draft}}. The mountain serves as the source of the {{ill|Sawakawa River|ja|沢川|12=draft}}, which flows into {{ill|Lake Momiji|ja|箕輪ダム|12=draft}} before joining the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}. The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩|12=draft}}, making it a geologically significant area where the {{ill|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|ja|糸魚川静岡構造線|12=draft}} intersects with the {{ill|Median Tectonic Line|ja|中央構造線|12=draft}}. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity {{ill|Moriya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}}, also identified with {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|12=draft}}. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Mountains associated with historical figures]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Mount Moriya]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[en:Moriya Mountain]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Moriya}}

{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 守屋山 |画像 = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] |画像キャプション = [[八ヶ岳]]の[[赤岳 (八ヶ岳山系)|赤岳]]から望む守屋山 |標高 = 1,650.3 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|58|03|N|138|05|36|E|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]・[[伊那市]] |山系 = [[伊那山地]] |種類 = |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}}守屋山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|58|03|138|05|36|守屋山|35.9675,138.0933|守屋山}} }} '''守屋山'''（もりやさん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]と[[伊那市]]との境にある[[標高]]1,651mの山である{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=標高値を改定する山岳一覧 資料1|publisher=国土地理院|accessdate=2014-03-26}}GNSS測量等の点検・補正調査による2014年4月1日の国土地理院『日本の山岳標高一覧-1003山-』における改定値。なお、旧版での標高は1,650m。。 == 名称 == 伝承によると旧名を「{{読み仮名|'''森山'''|もりやま}}」という。古文書には「'''守屋ヶ嶽'''」「'''守矢が岳'''」という名称も見られる太田亮 「第七章 祠官」『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 諏訪神社誌 第1巻]』官幣大社諏訪神社附属 諏訪明神講社、1926年、225, 227頁。。 諏訪地方の人々には守屋山を含めた伊那側の山並みを「西山」とも呼ばれている。 == 概要 == [[伊那山地]]の最北部にあり、山頂からは、[[赤石山脈|南アルプス]]、[[木曽山脈|中央アルプス]]、[[飛騨山脈|北アルプス]]、[[八ヶ岳連峰]]といった山々が眺望できる。[[清流]]とうたわれる[[沢川]]の水源で、もみじ湖（[[箕輪ダム]]の人造湖）を経て[[天竜川]]に注ぐ。 [[グリーンタフ|緑色凝灰岩]]でできているこの山は、[[糸魚川静岡構造線]]と[[中央構造線]]が交わる地点にあたり、地質学的にきわめて重要な地域である。 東峰には山頂から少し下ったところに石祠があり、古絵図には「守矢大臣宮」「守矢大神」という名で描かれている。現在は南麓にある守屋神社（[[伊那市]][[高遠町]]藤澤区片倉）の奥宮とされている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、133-137頁。。 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|[[杖突峠]]から見た[[八ヶ岳]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|山頂から見た[[諏訪湖]] 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|山頂直下のラビットハウス == 信仰 == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰）_-_Mononobe_no_Moriya_Shrine_(Mt._Moriya).jpg|サムネイル|200px|守屋神社奥宮と磐座（東峰）]] その名称から「モリヤ」という神（[[洩矢神]]あるいは[[物部守屋]]）が宿る山として信仰を集めた。 === 雨乞い信仰 === 守屋山の神が怒ると雨をもたらすと信じられ、過去には干天が続くと[[雨乞い]]として山頂の祠を谷底に突き落とす習慣があった。現在は祠が柵で囲ってあるのはこれを防ぐためである原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、141-143頁。。 「おじり{{efn|[[諏訪湖]]の尻、つまり[[釜口水門]]から[[天竜川]]へ流れ落ちる方面を指す。}}晴れ 守屋へ雲を 巻き上げて [[モズ|百舌鳥]]きち鳴かば 鎌を研ぐべし」という[[諺]]で言われているように、山頂に雲がかかると必ず雨が降ると信じられていたことから、[[諏訪盆地]]や伊那谷 に住む人々には古くから気象の予知に用いられた原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、140-141頁。。 === 磐座信仰 === 祠に向かって右側にある岩も[[磐座]]として信仰の対象であった。かつては守屋山の西側に住む人たちが[[6月 (旧暦)|旧6月]]朔日に登山して、祠を拝した後に磐座を7回まわって諏訪上社へ参拝するという行事を行い、これを「御七堂」と呼んだ原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、143頁。。 磐座の表面には文字らしきものが彫ってあり、祈雨や五穀豊穣を願うまじないの痕跡ではないかと思われる原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、144頁。。 === 諏訪大社上社との関係 === [[ファイル:Hokora in Mount Moriya (守屋山上の祠).png|left|サムネイル|200px|20世紀前半の東峰の祠の様子
[[宮地直一]]『諏訪史』（1931年）より]] 近年諏訪上社の神体山とされるが、もともと[[建御名方神|諏訪明神]]（[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]）の神体は[[諏訪氏]]出の[[諏訪大社#神職|大祝]]であり、歴史的に守屋山を神体とした記録はない。それどころか、山頂の石祠には[[御柱]]がなく、諏訪に背を向けている寺田鎮子、鷲尾徹太『諏訪明神―カミ信仰の原像』岩田書店、2010年、133頁。。ただし、[[宝治]]3年（1249年）に書かれたといわれる『[[諏訪信重解状]]』には、諏訪明神が守屋山の麓に降臨して、この地を治めていた[[ミシャグジ|守屋大臣]]（[[洩矢神]]）と[[洩矢神#明神入諏|覇権争い]]をした後、上社を構えたという伝承が書かれているため諏訪市史編纂委員会 編「第二節 諏訪神社上社・下社」『諏訪市史 上巻 (原始・古代・中世)』1995年、682-683頁。宮坂光昭『諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事』郷土出版社、1992年、91-93頁。原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、148-149頁。、必ずしも上社とは全く関係がないとは言えない。 [[天正]]10年（1582年）3月、[[織田信忠]]の軍勢が上社を焼き討ちした時、神官たちが[[神輿]]を担ぎ出し守屋山へ避難したと言われている。この事跡に関係する地名（御輿坂、権祝昼飯場など）が山中に残っている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、134-135頁。。 [[寛政]]12年（1800年）、[[白銅]]製の八稜鏡が山に発見され、天正の兵乱の際に紛失した宝物だろうということで上社に寄進された。この鏡は現在は上社本宮の宝物殿に収蔵されている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、135頁。。 御柱祭の時に建て替えられる2つの[[諏訪大社#神体|宝殿]]の網代天井の一部には、山中の一角に生えている「穂無し[[茅|萱]]」が古くから使用されている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、139頁。。 === 守矢氏と物部氏との関係 === [[ファイル:Jinchoukan-Moriya-shiryoukan Jinchoukan-ura-kofun.JPG|サムネイル|右|神長官裏古墳（[[茅野市]]）
守矢氏の屋敷内（現在の[[神長官守矢史料館]]）にある7世紀頃の古墳。守矢氏の言い伝えによると、この古墳は武麿（弟君）のものといわれている。]] 諏訪上社に{{読み仮名|神長|かんのおさ}}という筆頭職に就いた[[守矢氏]]の家伝によると、[[物部守屋]]の次男の武麿（{{読み仮名|弟君|おとぎみ}}とも）が[[丁未の乱]]の後、守屋山に逃れて、やがて守矢氏へ養子入りして神長となった原正直 「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』 山本ひろ子、2018年、149-154頁。[[大和岩雄]] 『信濃古代史考』 名著出版、1990年、110-111頁。。なお、守矢氏へ養子入りしたといわれる[[平忠度]]の子についてもまったく同じ伝承が語られている。これらには、神長となる者が[[通過儀礼]]として聖地とされた守屋山に籠っていた事が反映しているのではないかと指摘されている田中基「穴巣始と外来魂」『諏訪信仰の発生と展開』 古部族研究会編、人間社〈日本原初考 3〉、2018年、210-212頁。。 物部守屋を祀る守屋神社が立つ伊那市の片倉地区にも、物部守屋の子孫と名乗る守屋姓の家が多く存在する。また、山中には鍛冶技術を持った物部氏とは関係があると思われる「{{読み仮名|鋳物師|いもじ}}ヶ釜」の地名が残っている原正直 「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』 山本ひろ子、2018年、157-158頁。。 === 風の三郎信仰 === 諏訪上社一帯を描いた伝『天正の古図』（『天正のボロボロ絵図』とも{{Cite web|和書|date=2018-12-29|url=https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=343&seq=0|title=上社古図　天正のボロボロ絵図 |website=公益財団法人 八十二文化財団|accessdate=2018-12-29}}）から、山中には[[東日本]]に知られる風神「[[風神#日本の風神|風の三郎]]」を祀る「三郎宮」がかつてあったことが分かる。諏訪上社にはこの社に対する神事が見当たらないため、この痕跡は民間信仰跡だと推測される原正直 「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』 山本ひろ子、2018年、158-160頁。。 製鉄には風が必要とされたため、鋳物師ヶ釜にいたと思われる鍛冶屋や鋳物師が祀った風の神がそのルーツだったと考えられる。 == 周辺の山 == * [[入笠山]] * [[経ヶ岳 (長野県)|経ヶ岳]] * [[八ヶ岳]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|25em}} == 関連図書 == * 垣外富士男、津野祐次、中山秀幸『新・分県登山ガイド（改訂版） 15 長野県の山』山と渓谷社、2010年。ISBN 978-4-6350-2365-8。 * 諏訪市史編纂委員会 編『諏訪市史 上巻 (原始・古代・中世)』 、諏訪市、1995年。 * 寺田鎮子、鷲尾徹太『諏訪明神―カミ信仰の原像』岩田書店、2010年。ISBN 978-4-8729-4608-6。 * 徳久球雄、武内正、石井光造 編『三省堂 日本山名事典』[[三省堂]]、2004年。ISBN 978-4-3851-5404-6。 * [[日本山岳会]]『新日本山岳誌』[[ナカニシヤ出版]]、2005年。ISBN 4-7795-0000-1。 * 日本山岳会東海支部『東海・北陸の200秀山 下（東海・信州編）』[[中日新聞社]]、2009年。ISBN 978-4-8062-0599-9。 * 宮坂光昭『諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事』郷土出版社、1992年。ISBN 978-4-8766-3178-0。 * [[宮地直一]] 『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1076393 諏訪史 第2巻 前編]』信濃教育会諏訪部会、1931年。 * [[山本ひろ子]] 編『諏訪学』国書刊行会、2018年。ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3。 * 『改訂新版 名古屋周辺の山』山と渓谷社、2010年。ISBN 978-4-6351-8017-7。 == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪大社]] *[[杖突峠]] *[[物部守屋]] *[[洩矢神]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html 守屋山｜信州山岳ガイド（信濃毎日新聞社）] {{信州百名山}} {{諏訪信仰}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやさん}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:長野県の山]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:諏訪市の地理]] [[Category:伊那市の地理]] [[Category:1000メートル峰]] [[Category:物部守屋]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft|12=draft}} | Range = {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|12=draft|12=draft}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}} | Map Caption = Location of Mount Moriya }} '''Mount Moriya''' (守屋山) is a mountain with an elevation of 1,651 meters, located on the border between {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=Revised Elevation Values for Mountains|publisher=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|accessdate=2014-03-26}} == Name == The mountain was historically known as '''Moriya Mountain''' (森山). Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Locals in the Suwa region often refer to the mountain range that includes Mount Moriya as "Nishiyama" (西山), meaning "Western Mountains." == Overview == Situated at the northernmost part of the {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|12=draft|12=draft}}, the summit provides panoramic views of the {{ill|Southern Alps (Japan)|ja|赤石山脈|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Central Alps (Japan)|ja|木曽山脈|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Northern Alps (Japan)|ja|飛騨山脈|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳連峰|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mountain serves as the source of the {{ill|Sawakawa River|ja|沢川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which flows into {{ill|Lake Momiji|ja|箕輪ダム|12=draft|12=draft}} before joining the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩|12=draft|12=draft}}, making it a geologically significant area where the {{ill|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|ja|糸魚川静岡構造線|12=draft|12=draft}} intersects with the {{ill|Median Tectonic Line|ja|中央構造線|12=draft|12=draft}}. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft|12=draft}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity {{ill|Moriya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft|12=draft}}, also identified with {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Mountains associated with historical figures]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Mount Moriya]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[en:Moriya Mountain]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Moriya}}

{{Infobox Mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}

Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社}} in {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity {{ill|Moriya|ja|洩矢神}}, also identified with {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Mountains associated with historical figures]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Mount Moriya]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[en:Moriya Mountain]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mount Moriya}}

revision.comment: 53 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:守屋山 up to rev 104503592
revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 守屋山 |画像 = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] |画像キャプション = [[八ヶ岳]]の[[赤岳 (八ヶ岳山系)|赤岳]]から望む守屋山 |標高 = 1,650.3 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|58|03|N|138|05|36|E|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]・[[伊那市]] |山系 = [[伊那山地]] |種類 = |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}}守屋山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|58|03|138|05|36|守屋山|35.9675,138.0933|守屋山}} }} '''守屋山'''（もりやさん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]と[[伊那市]]との境にある[[標高]]1,651mの山である{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=標高値を改定する山岳一覧 資料1|publisher=国土地理院|accessdate=2014-03-26}}GNSS測量等の点検・補正調査による2014年4月1日の国土地理院『日本の山岳標高一覧-1003山-』における改定値。なお、旧版での標高は1,650m。。 == 名称 == 伝承によると旧名を「{{読み仮名|'''森山'''|もりやま}}」という。古文書には「'''守屋ヶ嶽'''」「'''守矢が岳'''」という名称も見られる太田亮 「第七章 祠官」『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 諏訪神社誌 第1巻]』官幣大社諏訪神社附属 諏訪明神講社、1926年、225, 227頁。。 諏訪地方の人々には守屋山を含めた伊那側の山並みを「西山」とも呼ばれている。 == 概要 == [[伊那山地]]の最北部にあり、山頂からは、[[赤石山脈|南アルプス]]、[[木曽山脈|中央アルプス]]、[[飛騨山脈|北アルプス]]、[[八ヶ岳連峰]]といった山々が眺望できる。[[清流]]とうたわれる[[沢川]]の水源で、もみじ湖（[[箕輪ダム]]の人造湖）を経て[[天竜川]]に注ぐ。 [[グリーンタフ|緑色凝灰岩]]でできているこの山は、[[糸魚川静岡構造線]]と[[中央構造線]]が交わる地点にあたり、地質学的にきわめて重要な地域である。 東峰には山頂から少し下ったところに石祠があり、古絵図には「守矢大臣宮」「守矢大神」という名で描かれている。現在は南麓にある守屋神社（[[伊那市]][[高遠町]]藤澤区片倉）の奥宮とされている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、133-137頁。。 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|[[杖突峠]]から見た[[八ヶ岳]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|山頂から見た[[諏訪湖]] 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|山頂直下のラビットハウス == 信仰 == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰）_-_Mononobe_no_Moriya_Shrine_(Mt._Moriya).jpg|サムネイル|200px|守屋神社奥宮と磐座（東峰）]] その名称から「モリヤ」という神（[[洩矢神]]あるいは[[物部守屋]]）が宿る山として信仰を集めた。 === 雨乞い信仰 === 守屋山の神が怒ると雨をもたらすと信じられ、過去には干天が続くと[[雨乞い]]として山頂の祠を谷底に突き落とす習慣があった。現在は祠が柵で囲ってあるのはこれを防ぐためである原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、141-143頁。。 「おじり{{efn|[[諏訪湖]]の尻、つまり[[釜口水門]]から[[天竜川]]へ流れ落ちる方面を指す。}}晴れ 守屋へ雲を 巻き上げて [[モズ|百舌鳥]]きち鳴かば 鎌を研ぐべし」という[[諺]]で言われているように、山頂に雲がかかると必ず雨が降ると信じられていたことから、[[諏訪盆地]]や伊那谷 に住む人々には古くから気象の予知に用いられた原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、140-141頁。。 === 磐座信仰 === 祠に向かって右側にある岩も[[磐座]]として信仰の対象であった。かつては守屋山の西側に住む人たちが[[6月 (旧暦)|旧6月]]朔日に登山して、祠を拝した後に磐座を7回まわって諏訪上社へ参拝するという行事を行い、これを「御七堂」と呼んだ原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、143頁。。 磐座の表面には文字らしきものが彫ってあり、祈雨や五穀豊穣を願うまじないの痕跡ではないかと思われる原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、144頁。。 === 諏訪大社上社との関係 === [[ファイル:Hokora in Mount Moriya (守屋山上の祠).png|left|サムネイル|200px|20世紀前半の東峰の祠の様子
[[宮地直一]]『諏訪史』（1931年）より]] 近年諏訪上社の神体山とされるが、もともと[[建御名方神|諏訪明神]]（[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]）の神体は[[諏訪氏]]出の[[諏訪大社#神職|大祝]]であり、歴史的に守屋山を神体とした記録はない。それどころか、山頂の石祠には[[御柱]]がなく、諏訪に背を向けている寺田鎮子、鷲尾徹太『諏訪明神―カミ信仰の原像』岩田書店、2010年、133頁。。ただし、[[宝治]]3年（1249年）に書かれたといわれる『[[諏訪信重解状]]』には、諏訪明神が守屋山の麓に降臨して、この地を治めていた[[ミシャグジ|守屋大臣]]（[[洩矢神]]）と[[洩矢神#明神入諏|覇権争い]]をした後、上社を構えたという伝承が書かれているため諏訪市史編纂委員会 編「第二節 諏訪神社上社・下社」『諏訪市史 上巻 (原始・古代・中世)』1995年、682-683頁。宮坂光昭『諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事』郷土出版社、1992年、91-93頁。原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、148-149頁。、必ずしも上社とは全く関係がないとは言えない。 [[天正]]10年（1582年）3月、[[織田信忠]]の軍勢が上社を焼き討ちした時、神官たちが[[神輿]]を担ぎ出し守屋山へ避難したと言われている。この事跡に関係する地名（御輿坂、権祝昼飯場など）が山中に残っている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、134-135頁。。 [[寛政]]12年（1800年）、[[白銅]]製の八稜鏡が山に発見され、天正の兵乱の際に紛失した宝物だろうということで上社に寄進された。この鏡は現在は上社本宮の宝物殿に収蔵されている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、135頁。。 御柱祭の時に建て替えられる2つの[[諏訪大社#神体|宝殿]]の網代天井の一部には、山中の一角に生えている「穂無し[[茅|萱]]」が古くから使用されている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、139頁。。 === 守矢氏と物部氏との関係 === [[ファイル:Jinchoukan-Moriya-shiryoukan Jinchoukan-ura-kofun.JPG|サムネイル|右|神長官裏古墳（[[茅野市]]）
守矢氏の屋敷内（現在の[[神長官守矢史料館]]）にある7世紀頃の古墳。守矢氏の言い伝えによると、この古墳は武麿（弟君）のものといわれている。]] 諏訪上社に{{読み仮名|神長|かんのおさ}}という筆頭職に就いた[[守矢氏]]の家伝によると、[[物部守屋]]の次男の武麿（{{読み仮名|弟君|おとぎみ}}とも）が[[丁未の乱]]の後、守屋山に逃れて、やがて守矢氏へ養子入りして神長となった原正直 「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』 山本ひろ子、2018年、149-154頁。[[大和岩雄]] 『信濃古代史考』 名著出版、1990年、110-111頁。。なお、守矢氏へ養子入りしたといわれる[[平忠度]]の子についてもまったく同じ伝承が語られている。これらには、神長となる者が[[通過儀礼]]として聖地とされた守屋山に籠っていた事が反映しているのではないかと指摘されている田中基「穴巣始と外来魂」『諏訪信仰の発生と展開』 古部族研究会編、人間社〈日本原初考 3〉、2018年、210-212頁。。 物部守屋を祀る守屋神社が立つ伊那市の片倉地区にも、物部守屋の子孫と名乗る守屋姓の家が多く存在する。また、山中には鍛冶技術を持った物部氏とは関係があると思われる「{{読み仮名|鋳物師|いもじ}}ヶ釜」の地名が残っている原正直 「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』 山本ひろ子、2018年、157-158頁。。 === 風の三郎信仰 === 諏訪上社一帯を描いた伝『天正の古図』（『天正のボロボロ絵図』とも{{Cite web|和書|date=2018-12-29|url=https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=343&seq=0|title=上社古図　天正のボロボロ絵図 |website=公益財団法人 八十二文化財団|accessdate=2018-12-29}}）から、山中には[[東日本]]に知られる風神「[[風神#日本の風神|風の三郎]]」を祀る「三郎宮」がかつてあったことが分かる。諏訪上社にはこの社に対する神事が見当たらないため、この痕跡は民間信仰跡だと推測される原正直 「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』 山本ひろ子、2018年、158-160頁。。 製鉄には風が必要とされたため、鋳物師ヶ釜にいたと思われる鍛冶屋や鋳物師が祀った風の神がそのルーツだったと考えられる。 == 周辺の山 == * [[入笠山]] * [[経ヶ岳 (長野県)|経ヶ岳]] * [[八ヶ岳]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|25em}} == 関連図書 == * 垣外富士男、津野祐次、中山秀幸『新・分県登山ガイド（改訂版） 15 長野県の山』山と渓谷社、2010年。ISBN 978-4-6350-2365-8。 * 諏訪市史編纂委員会 編『諏訪市史 上巻 (原始・古代・中世)』 、諏訪市、1995年。 * 寺田鎮子、鷲尾徹太『諏訪明神―カミ信仰の原像』岩田書店、2010年。ISBN 978-4-8729-4608-6。 * 徳久球雄、武内正、石井光造 編『三省堂 日本山名事典』[[三省堂]]、2004年。ISBN 978-4-3851-5404-6。 * [[日本山岳会]]『新日本山岳誌』[[ナカニシヤ出版]]、2005年。ISBN 4-7795-0000-1。 * 日本山岳会東海支部『東海・北陸の200秀山 下（東海・信州編）』[[中日新聞社]]、2009年。ISBN 978-4-8062-0599-9。 * 宮坂光昭『諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事』郷土出版社、1992年。ISBN 978-4-8766-3178-0。 * [[宮地直一]] 『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1076393 諏訪史 第2巻 前編]』信濃教育会諏訪部会、1931年。 * [[山本ひろ子]] 編『諏訪学』国書刊行会、2018年。ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3。 * 『改訂新版 名古屋周辺の山』山と渓谷社、2010年。ISBN 978-4-6351-8017-7。 == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪大社]] *[[杖突峠]] *[[物部守屋]] *[[洩矢神]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html 守屋山｜信州山岳ガイド（信濃毎日新聞社）] {{信州百名山}} {{諏訪信仰}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやさん}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:長野県の山]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:諏訪市の地理]] [[Category:伊那市の地理]] [[Category:1000メートル峰]] [[Category:物部守屋]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[守屋山]] to [[Mount Moriya]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 守屋山 |画像 = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] |画像キャプション = [[八ヶ岳]]の[[赤岳 (八ヶ岳山系)|赤岳]]から望む守屋山 |標高 = 1,650.3 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|58|03|N|138|05|36|E|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]・[[伊那市]] |山系 = [[伊那山地]] |種類 = |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}}守屋山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|58|03|138|05|36|守屋山|35.9675,138.0933|守屋山}} }} '''守屋山'''（もりやさん）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]と[[伊那市]]との境にある[[標高]]1,651mの山である{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=標高値を改定する山岳一覧 資料1|publisher=国土地理院|accessdate=2014-03-26}}GNSS測量等の点検・補正調査による2014年4月1日の国土地理院『日本の山岳標高一覧-1003山-』における改定値。なお、旧版での標高は1,650m。。 == 名称 == 伝承によると旧名を「{{読み仮名|'''森山'''|もりやま}}」という。古文書には「'''守屋ヶ嶽'''」「'''守矢が岳'''」という名称も見られる太田亮 「第七章 祠官」『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 諏訪神社誌 第1巻]』官幣大社諏訪神社附属 諏訪明神講社、1926年、225, 227頁。。 諏訪地方の人々には守屋山を含めた伊那側の山並みを「西山」とも呼ばれている。 == 概要 == [[伊那山地]]の最北部にあり、山頂からは、[[赤石山脈|南アルプス]]、[[木曽山脈|中央アルプス]]、[[飛騨山脈|北アルプス]]、[[八ヶ岳連峰]]といった山々が眺望できる。[[清流]]とうたわれる[[沢川]]の水源で、もみじ湖（[[箕輪ダム]]の人造湖）を経て[[天竜川]]に注ぐ。 [[グリーンタフ|緑色凝灰岩]]でできているこの山は、[[糸魚川静岡構造線]]と[[中央構造線]]が交わる地点にあたり、地質学的にきわめて重要な地域である。 東峰には山頂から少し下ったところに石祠があり、古絵図には「守矢大臣宮」「守矢大神」という名で描かれている。現在は南麓にある守屋神社（[[伊那市]][[高遠町]]藤澤区片倉）の奥宮とされている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、133-137頁。。 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|[[杖突峠]]から見た[[八ヶ岳]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|山頂から見た[[諏訪湖]] 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|山頂直下のラビットハウス == 信仰 == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰）_-_Mononobe_no_Moriya_Shrine_(Mt._Moriya).jpg|サムネイル|200px|守屋神社奥宮と磐座（東峰）]] その名称から「モリヤ」という神（[[洩矢神]]あるいは[[物部守屋]]）が宿る山として信仰を集めた。 === 雨乞い信仰 === 守屋山の神が怒ると雨をもたらすと信じられ、過去には干天が続くと[[雨乞い]]として山頂の祠を谷底に突き落とす習慣があった。現在は祠が柵で囲ってあるのはこれを防ぐためである原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、141-143頁。。 「おじり{{efn|[[諏訪湖]]の尻、つまり[[釜口水門]]から[[天竜川]]へ流れ落ちる方面を指す。}}晴れ 守屋へ雲を 巻き上げて [[モズ|百舌鳥]]きち鳴かば 鎌を研ぐべし」という[[諺]]で言われているように、山頂に雲がかかると必ず雨が降ると信じられていたことから、[[諏訪盆地]]や伊那谷 に住む人々には古くから気象の予知に用いられた原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、140-141頁。。 === 磐座信仰 === 祠に向かって右側にある岩も[[磐座]]として信仰の対象であった。かつては守屋山の西側に住む人たちが[[6月 (旧暦)|旧6月]]朔日に登山して、祠を拝した後に磐座を7回まわって諏訪上社へ参拝するという行事を行い、これを「御七堂」と呼んだ原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、143頁。。 磐座の表面には文字らしきものが彫ってあり、祈雨や五穀豊穣を願うまじないの痕跡ではないかと思われる原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、144頁。。 === 諏訪大社上社との関係 === [[ファイル:Hokora in Mount Moriya (守屋山上の祠).png|left|サムネイル|200px|20世紀前半の東峰の祠の様子
[[宮地直一]]『諏訪史』（1931年）より]] 近年諏訪上社の神体山とされるが、もともと[[建御名方神|諏訪明神]]（[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]）の神体は[[諏訪氏]]出の[[諏訪大社#神職|大祝]]であり、歴史的に守屋山を神体とした記録はない。それどころか、山頂の石祠には[[御柱]]がなく、諏訪に背を向けている寺田鎮子、鷲尾徹太『諏訪明神―カミ信仰の原像』岩田書店、2010年、133頁。。ただし、[[宝治]]3年（1249年）に書かれたといわれる『[[諏訪信重解状]]』には、諏訪明神が守屋山の麓に降臨して、この地を治めていた[[ミシャグジ|守屋大臣]]（[[洩矢神]]）と[[洩矢神#明神入諏|覇権争い]]をした後、上社を構えたという伝承が書かれているため諏訪市史編纂委員会 編「第二節 諏訪神社上社・下社」『諏訪市史 上巻 (原始・古代・中世)』1995年、682-683頁。宮坂光昭『諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事』郷土出版社、1992年、91-93頁。原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、148-149頁。、必ずしも上社とは全く関係がないとは言えない。 [[天正]]10年（1582年）3月、[[織田信忠]]の軍勢が上社を焼き討ちした時、神官たちが[[神輿]]を担ぎ出し守屋山へ避難したと言われている。この事跡に関係する地名（御輿坂、権祝昼飯場など）が山中に残っている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、134-135頁。。 [[寛政]]12年（1800年）、[[白銅]]製の八稜鏡が山に発見され、天正の兵乱の際に紛失した宝物だろうということで上社に寄進された。この鏡は現在は上社本宮の宝物殿に収蔵されている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、135頁。。 御柱祭の時に建て替えられる2つの[[諏訪大社#神体|宝殿]]の網代天井の一部には、山中の一角に生えている「穂無し[[茅|萱]]」が古くから使用されている原正直「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』山本ひろ子編、国書刊行会、2018年、139頁。。 === 守矢氏と物部氏との関係 === [[ファイル:Jinchoukan-Moriya-shiryoukan Jinchoukan-ura-kofun.JPG|サムネイル|右|神長官裏古墳（[[茅野市]]）
守矢氏の屋敷内（現在の[[神長官守矢史料館]]）にある7世紀頃の古墳。守矢氏の言い伝えによると、この古墳は武麿（弟君）のものといわれている。]] 諏訪上社に{{読み仮名|神長|かんのおさ}}という筆頭職に就いた[[守矢氏]]の家伝によると、[[物部守屋]]の次男の武麿（{{読み仮名|弟君|おとぎみ}}とも）が[[丁未の乱]]の後、守屋山に逃れて、やがて守矢氏へ養子入りして神長となった原正直 「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』 山本ひろ子、2018年、149-154頁。[[大和岩雄]] 『信濃古代史考』 名著出版、1990年、110-111頁。。なお、守矢氏へ養子入りしたといわれる[[平忠度]]の子についてもまったく同じ伝承が語られている。これらには、神長となる者が[[通過儀礼]]として聖地とされた守屋山に籠っていた事が反映しているのではないかと指摘されている田中基「穴巣始と外来魂」『諏訪信仰の発生と展開』 古部族研究会編、人間社〈日本原初考 3〉、2018年、210-212頁。。 物部守屋を祀る守屋神社が立つ伊那市の片倉地区にも、物部守屋の子孫と名乗る守屋姓の家が多く存在する。また、山中には鍛冶技術を持った物部氏とは関係があると思われる「{{読み仮名|鋳物師|いもじ}}ヶ釜」の地名が残っている原正直 「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』 山本ひろ子、2018年、157-158頁。。 === 風の三郎信仰 === 諏訪上社一帯を描いた伝『天正の古図』（『天正のボロボロ絵図』とも{{Cite web|和書|date=2018-12-29|url=https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=343&seq=0|title=上社古図　天正のボロボロ絵図 |website=公益財団法人 八十二文化財団|accessdate=2018-12-29}}）から、山中には[[東日本]]に知られる風神「[[風神#日本の風神|風の三郎]]」を祀る「三郎宮」がかつてあったことが分かる。諏訪上社にはこの社に対する神事が見当たらないため、この痕跡は民間信仰跡だと推測される原正直 「守屋山の習俗と伝承」『諏訪学』 山本ひろ子、2018年、158-160頁。。 製鉄には風が必要とされたため、鋳物師ヶ釜にいたと思われる鍛冶屋や鋳物師が祀った風の神がそのルーツだったと考えられる。 == 周辺の山 == * [[入笠山]] * [[経ヶ岳 (長野県)|経ヶ岳]] * [[八ヶ岳]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|25em}} == 関連図書 == * 垣外富士男、津野祐次、中山秀幸『新・分県登山ガイド（改訂版） 15 長野県の山』山と渓谷社、2010年。ISBN 978-4-6350-2365-8。 * 諏訪市史編纂委員会 編『諏訪市史 上巻 (原始・古代・中世)』 、諏訪市、1995年。 * 寺田鎮子、鷲尾徹太『諏訪明神―カミ信仰の原像』岩田書店、2010年。ISBN 978-4-8729-4608-6。 * 徳久球雄、武内正、石井光造 編『三省堂 日本山名事典』[[三省堂]]、2004年。ISBN 978-4-3851-5404-6。 * [[日本山岳会]]『新日本山岳誌』[[ナカニシヤ出版]]、2005年。ISBN 4-7795-0000-1。 * 日本山岳会東海支部『東海・北陸の200秀山 下（東海・信州編）』[[中日新聞社]]、2009年。ISBN 978-4-8062-0599-9。 * 宮坂光昭『諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事』郷土出版社、1992年。ISBN 978-4-8766-3178-0。 * [[宮地直一]] 『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1076393 諏訪史 第2巻 前編]』信濃教育会諏訪部会、1931年。 * [[山本ひろ子]] 編『諏訪学』国書刊行会、2018年。ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3。 * 『改訂新版 名古屋周辺の山』山と渓谷社、2010年。ISBN 978-4-6351-8017-7。 == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪大社]] *[[杖突峠]] *[[物部守屋]] *[[洩矢神]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html 守屋山｜信州山岳ガイド（信濃毎日新聞社）] {{信州百名山}} {{諏訪信仰}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやさん}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:長野県の山]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:諏訪市の地理]] [[Category:伊那市の地理]] [[Category:1000メートル峰]] [[Category:物部守屋]]

{{Infobox Mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市}} | Range = {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}} | Map Caption = Location of Mount Moriya }} '''Mount Moriya''' (守屋山) is a mountain with an elevation of 1,651 meters, located on the border between {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}} and {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}.{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=Revised Elevation Values for Mountains|publisher=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|accessdate=2014-03-26}} == Name == The mountain was historically known as '''Moriya Mountain''' (森山). Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Locals in the Suwa region often refer to the mountain range that includes Mount Moriya as "Nishiyama" (西山), meaning "Western Mountains." == Overview == Situated at the northernmost part of the {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地}}, the summit provides panoramic views of the {{ill|Southern Alps (Japan)|ja|赤石山脈}}, {{ill|Central Alps (Japan)|ja|木曽山脈}}, {{ill|Northern Alps (Japan)|ja|飛騨山脈}}, and {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳連峰}}. The mountain serves as the source of the {{ill|Sawakawa River|ja|沢川}}, which flows into {{ill|Lake Momiji|ja|箕輪ダム}} before joining the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川}}. The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩}}, making it a geologically significant area where the {{ill|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|ja|糸魚川静岡構造線}} intersects with the {{ill|Median Tectonic Line|ja|中央構造線}}. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社}} in {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity {{ill|Moriya|ja|洩矢神}}, also identified with {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋}}. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. {{Infobox Mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft}} | Range = {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|12=draft}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}} | Map Caption = Location of Mount Moriya }} '''Mount Moriya''' (守屋山) is a mountain with an elevation of 1,651 meters, located on the border between {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}.{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=Revised Elevation Values for Mountains|publisher=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|accessdate=2014-03-26}} == Name == The mountain was historically known as '''Moriya Mountain''' (森山). Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Locals in the Suwa region often refer to the mountain range that includes Mount Moriya as "Nishiyama" (西山), meaning "Western Mountains." == Overview == Situated at the northernmost part of the {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|12=draft}}, the summit provides panoramic views of the {{ill|Southern Alps (Japan)|ja|赤石山脈|12=draft}}, {{ill|Central Alps (Japan)|ja|木曽山脈|12=draft}}, {{ill|Northern Alps (Japan)|ja|飛騨山脈|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳連峰|12=draft}}. The mountain serves as the source of the {{ill|Sawakawa River|ja|沢川|12=draft}}, which flows into {{ill|Lake Momiji|ja|箕輪ダム|12=draft}} before joining the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}. The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩|12=draft}}, making it a geologically significant area where the {{ill|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|ja|糸魚川静岡構造線|12=draft}} intersects with the {{ill|Median Tectonic Line|ja|中央構造線|12=draft}}. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|12=draft}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|守屋山|version=104503592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Mountains associated with historical figures]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Mount Moriya]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[en:Moriya Mountain]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{Infobox Mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina, Nagano|qq=}} | Range = {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Mountains|qq=}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}} | Map Caption = Location of Mount Moriya }} '''Mount Moriya''' (守屋山) is a mountain with an elevation of 1,651 meters, located on the border between {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa, Nagano|qq=}} and {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina, Nagano|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}.{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=Revised Elevation Values for Mountains|publisher=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|accessdate=2014-03-26}} == Name == The mountain was historically known as '''Moriya Mountain''' (森山). Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Locals in the Suwa region often refer to the mountain range that includes Mount Moriya as "Nishiyama" (西山), meaning "Western Mountains." == Overview == Situated at the northernmost part of the {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Mountains|qq=}}, the summit provides panoramic views of the {{ill|Southern Alps (Japan)|ja|赤石山脈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Alps (Japan)|qq=}}, {{ill|Central Alps (Japan)|ja|木曽山脈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Central Alps (Japan)|qq=}}, {{ill|Northern Alps (Japan)|ja|飛騨山脈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Alps (Japan)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳連峰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugatake Mountains|qq=}}. The mountain serves as the source of the {{ill|Sawakawa River|ja|沢川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawakawa River|qq=}}, which flows into {{ill|Lake Momiji|ja|箕輪ダム|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Momiji|qq=}} before joining the {{ill|Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryu River|qq=}}. The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/green tuff|qq=}}, making it a geologically significant area where the {{ill|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|ja|糸魚川静岡構造線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|qq=}} intersects with the {{ill|Median Tectonic Line|ja|中央構造線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Median Tectonic Line|qq=}}. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina, Nagano|qq=}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity {{ill|Moriya|ja|洩矢神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya|qq=}}, also identified with {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Moriya|qq=}}. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nyukasa|qq=}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kyogatake|qq=}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugatake Mountains|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|守屋山|version=104503592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Mountains associated with historical figures]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Mount Moriya]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[en:Moriya Mountain]]

{{Infobox Mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina, Nagano|qq=}} | Range = {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Mountains|qq=}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}} | Map Caption = Location of Mount Moriya }} '''Mount Moriya''' (守屋山) is a mountain with an elevation of 1,651 meters, located on the border between {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa, Nagano|qq=}} and {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina, Nagano|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}.{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=Revised Elevation Values for Mountains|publisher=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|accessdate=2014-03-26}} == Name == The mountain was historically known as '''Moriya Mountain''' (森山). Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Locals in the Suwa region often refer to the mountain range that includes Mount Moriya as "Nishiyama" (西山), meaning "Western Mountains." == Overview == Situated at the northernmost part of the {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Mountains|qq=}}, the summit provides panoramic views of the {{ill|Southern Alps (Japan)|ja|赤石山脈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Alps (Japan)|qq=}}, {{ill|Central Alps (Japan)|ja|木曽山脈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Central Alps (Japan)|qq=}}, {{ill|Northern Alps (Japan)|ja|飛騨山脈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Alps (Japan)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳連峰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugatake Mountains|qq=}}. The mountain serves as the source of the {{ill|Sawakawa River|ja|沢川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawakawa River|qq=}}, which flows into {{ill|Lake Momiji|ja|箕輪ダム|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Momiji|qq=}} before joining the {{ill|Tenryū River|lt=Tenryū River|lt=Tenryu River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryu River|qq=}}. The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/green tuff|qq=}}, making it a geologically significant area where the {{ill|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|ja|糸魚川静岡構造線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|qq=}} intersects with the {{ill|Median Tectonic Line|ja|中央構造線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Median Tectonic Line|qq=}}. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ina, Nagano|ja|伊那市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina, Nagano|qq=}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity {{ill|Moriya|ja|洩矢神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya|qq=}}, also identified with {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Moriya|qq=}}. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * {{ill|Mount Nyukasa|ja|入笠山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nyukasa|qq=}} * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kyogatake|qq=}} * {{ill|Yatsugatake Mountains|ja|八ヶ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsugatake Mountains|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|守屋山|version=104503592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[en:Moriya Mountain]]

{{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Ina, Nagano|Ina, Nagano]] | Range = {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Mountains|qq=}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}} | Map Caption = Location of Mount Moriya }} '''Mount Moriya''' (守屋山) is a mountain with an elevation of 1,651 meters, located on the border between [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]] and [[:en:Ina, Nagano|Ina, Nagano]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=Revised Elevation Values for Mountains|publisher=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|accessdate=2014-03-26}} == Name == The mountain was historically known as '''Moriya Mountain''' (森山). Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Locals in the Suwa region often refer to the mountain range that includes Mount Moriya as "Nishiyama" (西山), meaning "Western Mountains." == Overview == Situated at the northernmost part of the {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Mountains|qq=}}, the summit provides panoramic views of the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Southern Alps (Japan)]], [[:en:Kiso Mountains|Central Alps (Japan)]], [[:en:Hida Mountains|Northern Alps (Japan)]], and [[:en:Yatsugatake Mountains|Yatsugatake Mountains]]. The mountain serves as the source of the {{ill|Sawakawa River|ja|沢川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawakawa River|qq=}}, which flows into [[:en:Minowa Dam|Lake Momiji]] before joining the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryu River]]. The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/green tuff|qq=}}, making it a geologically significant area where the [[:en:Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line]] intersects with the [[:en:Japan Median Tectonic Line|Median Tectonic Line]]. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ina, Nagano|Ina, Nagano]].Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity [[:en:Moreya|Moriya]], also identified with [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * [[:en:Mount Nyukasa|Mount Nyukasa]] * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kyogatake|qq=}} * [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake Mountains]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|守屋山|version=104503592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[en:Moriya Mountain]]

The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/green tuff|qq=}}, making it a geologically significant area where the [[:en:Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line]] intersects with the [[:en:Japan Median Tectonic Line|Median Tectonic Line]]. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ina, Nagano|Ina, Nagano]].Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity [[:en:Moreya|Moriya]], also identified with [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * [[:en:Mount Nyukasa|Mount Nyukasa]] * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kyogatake|qq=}} * [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake Mountains]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity [[:en:Moreya|Moriya]], also identified with [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity [[:en:Moreya|Moriya]], also identified with [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. {{Infobox mountain | Name = Mount Moriya | image = [[File:Mt.Moriya from Mt.Akadake.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View of Mount Moriya from [[Mount Akadake (Yatsugatake)|Mount Akadake]] of the [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] | Elevation_m = 1,650.3 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35.9675|138.0933|type:mountain_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{JPN}}
[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], [[:en:Ina, Nagano|Ina, Nagano]] | Range = {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Mountains|qq=}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.9675|138.0933}}|caption=|width=300}} | Map Caption = Location of Mount Moriya }} '''Mount Moriya''' (守屋山) is a mountain with an elevation of 1,651 meters, located on the border between [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]] and [[:en:Ina, Nagano|Ina, Nagano]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/common/000091072.pdf|title=Revised Elevation Values for Mountains|publisher=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|accessdate=2014-03-26}} == Name == The mountain was historically known as '''Moriya Mountain''' (森山). Historical records also refer to it as '''Moriya Peak''' (守屋ヶ嶽) or '''Moriya Ridge''' (守矢が岳).Ota, Ryo, "[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/983470/122 Suwa Shrine Records Vol. 1]," 1926, pp. 225, 227. Locals in the Suwa region often refer to the mountain range that includes Mount Moriya as "Nishiyama" (西山), meaning "Western Mountains." == Overview == Situated at the northernmost part of the {{ill|Ina Mountains|ja|伊那山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Mountains|qq=}}, the summit provides panoramic views of the [[:en:Akaishi Mountains|Southern Alps (Japan)]], [[:en:Kiso Mountains|Central Alps (Japan)]], [[:en:Hida Mountains|Northern Alps (Japan)]], and [[:en:Yatsugatake Mountains|Yatsugatake Mountains]]. The mountain serves as the source of the {{ill|Sawakawa River|ja|沢川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawakawa River|qq=}}, which flows into [[:en:Minowa Dam|Lake Momiji]] before joining the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryu River]]. The mountain is composed primarily of {{ill|green tuff|ja|緑色凝灰岩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/green tuff|qq=}}, making it a geologically significant area where the [[:en:Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line|Itoigawa-Shizuoka Tectonic Line]] intersects with the [[:en:Japan Median Tectonic Line|Median Tectonic Line]]. On the eastern peak, a stone shrine—referred to in ancient maps as the "Moriya Great Minister Shrine" (守矢大臣宮)—is associated with {{ill|Moriya Shrine|ja|守屋神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ina, Nagano|Ina, Nagano]].Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity [[:en:Moreya|Moriya]], also identified with [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * [[:en:Mount Nyukasa|Mount Nyukasa]] * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kyogatake|qq=}} * [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake Mountains]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|守屋山|version=104503592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:1000-meter summits]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[Category:One-thousanders]] [[Category:Geography of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:Mononobe no Moriya]] [[Category:Geography of Suwa]] {{Commons category|Mount Moriya (Nagano)}} [[lld:Mont Moriya]]

Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 133–137. 杖突峠から見た南八ヶ岳.jpg|View of [[Yatsugatake Mountains]] from [[Tsurugatoge Pass]] 守屋山山頂から見た諏訪湖.jpg|View of [[Lake Suwa]] from the summit 守屋山山頂直下のラビットハウス.jpg|Rabbit House near the summit == Belief and Worship == [[File:守屋神社奥宮（守屋山東峰） - Mononobe no Moriya Shrine (Mt. Moriya).jpg|thumb|200px|The inner shrine and sacred rock at the eastern peak]] Mount Moriya has long been revered as the abode of the deity [[:en:Moreya|Moriya]], also identified with [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]]. === Rain Rituals === It is believed that when the deity of Mount Moriya is angered, rain will follow. In times of drought, locals performed rituals involving throwing the summit shrine into the valley, a practice now prevented by fences.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 141–143. The saying, "When clouds gather on Moriya and the shrike cries, it’s time to sharpen the sickle," reflects the traditional use of the mountain for weather forecasting.Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, pp. 140–141. === Sacred Rock Worship === The rock adjacent to the shrine was also venerated as a sacred site. Ancient ceremonies involved circling this rock seven times and then visiting [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]].Hara, Masanao, "Customs and Legends of Mount Moriya," in ''Suwa Studies'', ed. Hiroko Yamamoto, Kokusho Kankokai, 2018, p. 143. == Related Mountains == * [[:en:Mount Nyukasa|Mount Nyukasa]] * {{ill|Mount Kyogatake|ja|経ヶ岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kyogatake|qq=}} * [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake Mountains]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hiroko Yamamoto, ''Suwa Studies'', Kokusho Kankokai, 2018. ISBN 978-4-3360-6254-3. == External Links == * [https://www8.shinmai.co.jp/yama/guide/00063.html Mount Moriya Guide] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|守屋山|version=104503592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sacred mountains]] [[Category:Mountain ranges of Japan]] [[Category:Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:守屋山]] [[sn:Mount Moriya]] [[ld:Mont Moriya]] [[Category:One-thousanders]] [[Category:Geography of Ina, Nagano]] [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:Mononobe no Moriya]] [[Category:Geography of Suwa]] {{Commons category|Mount Moriya (Nagano)}} [[lld:Mont Moriya]]
